prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model for stock prices. Key assumptions include an estimated annual drift (average return) of 12% and an annual volatility of 30%. These parameters are generalized estimates due to the lack of real-time historical data access for VRTX. The price is projected over 20 trading days (approximately 0.079 years). The point estimate represents the expected price (mean of the log-normal distribution), and the confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution. Stock price movements are highly unpredictable, and actual future prices could fall outside these intervals.
